prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Friday lunchtime: 10, Monday morning: 10, Saturday night: 10, Sunday night: 10, Wednesday evening: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Earth: 10, Mars: 10, Mercury: 10, Saturn: 10, Venus: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:

prompt:

You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Mars: 10, Saturn: 10, Venus: 10, Earth: 10, Mercury: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Ford: 10, Mercedes: 10, Pontiac: 10, Porsche: 10, Volkswagen: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: 10: 10, 15: 10, 1: 10, 2: 10, 7: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: deck chair: 10, dining chair: 10, easy chair: 10, rocking chair: 10, stool: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: stool: 10, rocking chair: 10, deck chair: 10, easy chair: 10, dining chair: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: easy chair: 10, stool: 10, dining chair: 10, rocking chair: 10, deck chair: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: jogging: 10, sitting: 10, sunbathing: 10, swimming: 10, walking: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: sunbathing: 10, jogging: 10, walking: 10, sitting: 10, swimming: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: walking: 10, swimming: 10, jogging: 10, sunbathing: 10, sitting: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.

 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.

If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: David: 10, John: 10, Michael: 10, Robert: 10, Steven: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: Steven: 10, Robert: 10, Michael: 10, David: 10, John: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

prompt:
In this task, you have to choose one of the following options. This is the list of options and their score: win nothing: 10, win trophy: 10, win chocolate: 10, win champagne: 10, win money: 10. You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.

If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.

If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

You will earn the number of points shown on the option you choose. You have been paired with another LLM and you have to guess what it chooses. If you guess correctly, you will receive the number of points indicated on that option. If not, you will receive nothing for that task. There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:
 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

There are four intuitive properties that make a choice desirable:

If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.
